metadata.description: Tools for turning scientific models into interactive, explorable GUIs.

plugins.name: model-scope
plugins.description: Hand it a paper and get a simulator: /model-scope:from-paper reads ONLY the reference, interviews you about the visualization purpose + specific requests, then builds the interactive simulation GUI and serves it at a verified local URL (with an optional shareable tunnel/permanent deploy when tooling allows). Or turn ANY parameterised model into an interactive explorer: move a slider and watch the simulation update, shown in views the MODEL defines (no fixed graphic/axis) — Bayesian/ideal-observer, efficient-coding & sequential observers, causal inference, working memory, neuron, decision (drift-diffusion), RL, POMDP, saccade, population, or any Monte-Carlo process. Philosophy: decompose the model's process to the ATOMIC level and show input → transformation → output at three zoom levels via a level switch (lenses): ⚛ Step (one update split into signal/leak/noise), ◷ Trial (the atom over time → an output), ∑ Simulation (many trials → the statistics) — rather than reproducing a paper figure. Idioms: a continuous playhead, a process-mode stage stepper, and the three-level lens switch. Build a whole paper as a few screens (mechanism → condition comparisons → prediction), separating conditions from parameters and surfacing the claim↔mechanism mapping; heavy screens stream via runChunks + a loading overlay. Schema-driven sliders + a simulate()→views contract + a small plotting helper, no build step. Includes a modelbook of canonical model families (grounded in real open source), a reusable code library (mslib.js), and a GUI QC pipeline.
